widgetData.source_sentence:
Semicolons in Emmet abbreviations inside SASS files <!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- Also please test using the latest insiders build to make sure your issue has not already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->

<!-- Use Help > Report Issue to prefill these. -->
- VSCode Version: 1.53.0
- OS Version: Windows 10 Pro 20H2

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open SASS file.
2. Expand any Emmet abbreviation.
3. The expanded line ends with a semicolon, which is unacceptable for SASS.
4. The issue appeared after 1.53.0 update.

<!-- Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes/No
sentences:
Breakpoint decorations go to wrong place - Open menuService.ts
- Set some breakpoints
- They show in the wrong place and cover a character, but seemingly get fixed when setting a breakpoint on the next line

![Recording 2022-04-12 at 16 31 15](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/323878/163071018-1265f8bc-ec1f-40af-9962-6695e14b2ad7.gif)
Breadcrumbs/selectbox dropdown does not relayout after resizing panel <!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- Also please test using the latest insiders build to make sure your issue has not already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->

<!-- Use Help > Report Issue to prefill these. -->
- VSCode Version: stable and insiders

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Click on a breadcrumb/selectbox so the dropdown shows up
2. Resize panel
3. :bug: Observe the dropdown does not relayout 

![breadcrumbsfloating](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/25115070/103451019-a1e1e300-4c8c-11eb-90ae-c2466029e713.gif)
![customselectboxfloating](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/25115070/103474190-55bb9f00-4d6f-11eb-98b2-77fdd40e1ff2.gif)

Terminal Sticky Scroll disappears when the beginning (not the end) of code block reaches the end of the scope Testing #199240

So I was testing the terminal sticky scroll and I noticed that the sticky code block disappears in an abrupt manner when the beginning (not the end) of the multiline terminal command, reaches the end of the scope. To give more context about what I mean, I added the GIF below. I was sort of expecting the code block to start disappearing when the line `>  `  reached the end of the output for that command. I noticed that the code block disappeared however when the line `echo hi; \` reached the end of the output for that run.

https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/assets/61460952/8c92b925-f29a-498a-a8f0-cd438e6d6c6c

I don't think this is a problem per say, it's a different manner of animating sticky scroll. I was just wondering if this is intended? I think the potential issue with this approach is that the sticky scroll block is hiding the next terminal command and its output while you are scrolling until it disappears. In the editor, sticky scroll starts disappearing progressively when the bottom of the last block/line touches the end of the scope. Perhaps we could explore something similar?

https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/assets/61460952/e3c8fdf2-7dc1-4363-a9f7-ff403a36f533

widgetData.source_sentence: announce what is in the input box Type: Feature Request When using the command palette with control + shift + p or searching for files with control + p, it is not possible to spell with right or left arrows what is being typed or what has been pasted. This would make it easier to correct what was typed in these areas or change parts after pasting something. I emphasize that the adjustment to be able to spell should not change the behavior of using up or down arrows to navigate suggestions as it currently works. Thank you very much in advance. VS Code version: Code - Insiders 1.71.0-insider (235a92a3693176fbf9af16ec5b829c458a1d682f, 2022-08-22T05:16:19.478Z) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.19044 Modes: Sandboxed: Yes Remote OS version: Linux x64 5.10.16.3-microsoft-standard-WSL2
sentences:
Cleared terminal content comes back after switching between sessions Type: Bug 1. Print something in integrated terminal 2. Right click, Clear 3. Create a new terminal session 4. Switch back to previous terminal session 🐛: Cleared terminal content comes back https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1330321/208982497-007cb17e-a2e8-490f-9753-569e60758522.mp4 VS Code version: Code - Insiders 1.75.0-insider (c437ec2497ccd6b5ff5b2f6da86a8042e1ed951a, 2022-12-21T05:41:44.328Z) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.25267 Modes: Sandboxed: Yes

Extensions (26) Extension|Author (truncated)|Version ---|---|--- Bookmarks|ale|13.3.1 gitlens|eam|13.2.0 prettier-vscode|esb|9.10.3 copilot-nightly|Git|1.65.7751 remotehub|Git|0.49.2022121901 vscode-env|Iro|0.1.0 search-node-modules|jas|1.3.0 fluent-icons|mig|0.0.18 remote-containers|ms-|0.268.0 remote-ssh|ms-|0.95.2022121915 remote-ssh-edit|ms-|0.84.0 remote-wsl|ms-|0.72.0 hexeditor|ms-|1.9.9 js-debug-nightly|ms-|2022.12.1417 remote-explorer|ms-|0.1.2022121209 remote-repositories|ms-|0.26.0 vscode-typescript-next|ms-|5.0.202212200 abracadabra|nic|6.17.0 material-icon-theme|PKi|4.23.0 prisma|Pri|4.8.0 vscode-yaml|red|1.10.1 reveal|smu|1.2.7 workspace-config-plus|Swe|0.2.2 luna-paint|Tyr|0.16.0 markdown-all-in-one|yzh|3.5.0 material-theme|zhu|3.15.6
explorer display folder is symbolic incorrectly https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/41773861/228953537-db765845-7f47-41e1-978d-56eda8199f0e.mov reproduce steps: 1. git clone git@github.com:tjx666/module-resolution.git 2. git reset --hard 126808cb72b1cfa5d1f0d0c6a3f25ed69491f536 3. pnpm install 4. open apps/esm-app/node_modules, you can see vscode show the folder `commonjs` is a symbolic link 5. delete folder apps/esm-app/node_modules/commonjs 6. copy packages/commonjs to folder apps/esm-app/node_modules/commonjs 7. the vscode still show folder apps/esm-app/node_modules/commonjs is a symbolic link, but actually not Command "Terminal: Clear" and Ctrl+Left make cursur jump to bottom Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes - VS Code Version: 1.74.0-insider - OS Version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.19044 Steps to Reproduce: 1. Click menu item "Terminal > New Terminal". 2. Run `echo foo`. 3. Hold Enter to fill the terminal view. 4. Run command `Terminal: Clear` from the command palette. 5. Press up-arrow key to fill the current line with the command `echo foo` again. Don't run it. 6. Press Ctrl+left-arrow key. The cursor jumps to the last line in the terminal view, which is empty, even though we were in the first line. 7. (Hitting Enter runs the command and fills the whole terminal view again.)

widgetData.source_sentence: Settings editor: focus border is cut off cc @roblourens
sentences:
Drag and drop in notebooks causes unexpected scrolling Testing #119715 (using `"notebook.experimental.useMarkdownRenderer": false`) Moving cells via drag and drops makes the scroll bar act a little unexpectedly. I think the behavior I expect to to keep ensure scrollTop remains identical after dropping. ![recording (13)](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2193314/112287874-0be5a500-8c4a-11eb-8f09-1867ee2e9d33.gif) VSCode Terminal History Being Set To Local Directory History After Update After a recent update of VSCode - Insiders, I'm getting local terminal history instead of global history when using Zsh. Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes - VS Code Version: code-insiders 1.74.0-insider (Universal) - OS Version: macOS 12.6 Steps to Reproduce: 1. open VSCode - Insiders 2. open the VSCode - Insiders integrated terminal using Zsh 3. type `history | wc -l` 4. open a regular macOS terminal 5. type `history | wc -l` Expected: I expected to see the global history within the VS Code - Insiders integrated terminal. Actual: The VS Code - Insiders integrated terminal is showing only local history. The history within the VSCode - Insiders integrated terminal should display the same global history as it appears in the normal macOS terminal. Emmet Expansion: Count down ($@-) doesn't work Using a multiplier with the count down syntax, ($@-), produces incorrect results. Per the example on [docs.emmet.io](https://docs.emmet.io/cheat-sheet/ ) under `Item numbering: $`: `ul>li.item$@-*5` should expand to: ```
``` Actual output: ```
```

sentences:
Source control shows empty original file in side-by-side comparison when project is located on file share Issue Type: Bug 1. Clone a git repository on a remote file share 2. Open folder \\\remote_system\project_folder 3. Edit one of the files 4. Open source control pane 5. Select modified file Expected result: Side-by-side comparison opens showing changes Actual result: Side-by-side comparison opens but the original file is empty and all lines in the file are marked as new. Running git diff in terminal on the file does show the modified lines. Tabs aren't aligned * move the sash back and worth * 🧀 the tabs aren't left aligned anymore Opening quick text search with a selection should select the text in the input by default Steps to Reproduce: * set up a keybinding to launch quick text search * open a diff editor * select some text * launch quick text search Expected: The text to be highlighted: ![Image](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/assets/2644648/d6b43f53-b25f-468d-9174-155b748027fb) Actual: The text is not highlighted: ![Image](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/assets/2644648/87750492-5731-4c8a-854d-2459a8d0a8e3) Highlighting it is good because if I _don't_ want my selection to be used, I just type over it. This aligns with the find widget: ![Image](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/assets/2644648/80bc6a78-591f-4b5d-a34c-42318c0fa4a3)

widgetData.source_sentence:
Shell integration: bash and zsh don't serialize \n and ; characters Part of https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/155639

Repro:

1. Open a bash or zsh session
2. Run:
  ```sh
  echo "a
  … b"
  ```
  ![image](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/assets/2193314/95ce755b-cec5-4da1-8791-75923a088efa)
3. ctrl+alt+r to run recent command, select the last command, 🐛 it's run without the new line
  ![image](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/assets/2193314/98e81851-3a68-422c-94e8-e3e4bb21ef53)
sentences:
TreeView state out of sync Testing #117304

Repro: Not Sure

Test state shows passed in file but still running in tree view.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6561887/108932683-a082c600-75fe-11eb-8ccd-afa5d0e2a91e.png)
rename terminal in new window says no argument provided Testing #196195



https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/assets/29464607/43eb0432-9ca4-435d-ad9c-ed084bb93f5a



Setting icon and color in createTerminal API no longer works correctly See https://github.com/fabiospampinato/vscode-terminals/issues/77

Looks like the default tab color/icon change probably regressed this.

Tabs aren't aligned * move the sash back and worth * 🧀 the tabs aren't left aligned anymore Opening quick text search with a selection should select the text in the input by default Steps to Reproduce: * set up a keybinding to launch quick text search * open a diff editor * select some text * launch quick text search Expected: The text to be highlighted: ![Image](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/assets/2644648/d6b43f53-b25f-468d-9174-155b748027fb) Actual: The text is not highlighted: ![Image](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/assets/2644648/87750492-5731-4c8a-854d-2459a8d0a8e3) Highlighting it is good because if I _don't_ want my selection to be used, I just type over it. This aligns with the find widget: ![Image](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/assets/2644648/80bc6a78-591f-4b5d-a34c-42318c0fa4a3)

widget.source_sentence:
Shell integration: bash and zsh don't serialize \n and ; characters Part of https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/155639

Repro:

1. Open a bash or zsh session
2. Run:
  ```sh
  echo "a
  … b"
  ```
  ![image](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/assets/2193314/95ce755b-cec5-4da1-8791-75923a088efa)
3. ctrl+alt+r to run recent command, select the last command, 🐛 it's run without the new line
  ![image](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/assets/2193314/98e81851-3a68-422c-94e8-e3e4bb21ef53)
sentences:

Looks like the default tab color/icon change probably regressed this.
